threads.title: The Vandy Renaissance
threads.dek: Vanderbilt isn't supposed to do this. Clark Lea, Diego Pavia, and the slow rise of the program nobody saw coming — except, apparently, the people in Nashville who never stopped watching.

latest_chapter.title: Spring 2026: Lea's Third Class, the Post-Pavia Quarterback, and the Renaissance's Structural Test
latest_chapter.dek: The Pavia era has closed. The next era's quarterback is being built in spring practice. The 2026 season is the first one in three years where the program's success is not contingent on a single transfer's chip on his shoulder.

threads.title: Coaching Carousel · 2026-27
threads.dek: The hot seats, the open jobs, the candidates that won't say they're candidates. The annual reshuffle that reshapes the sport, mapped chapter by chapter as it happens.

latest_chapter.title: Spring 2026: Drinkwitz at Auburn, Franklin at Florida, and the Early Evidence on Whether the Hires Are Producing
latest_chapter.dek: Three months in. Drinkwitz is closing on the recruiting class Auburn needed. Franklin is rebuilding Florida's class while installing his program. Diaz at Penn State is mostly invisible, which is what year one of any rebuild should look like.

threads.title: The Big Ten Reasserting
threads.dek: After two decades of SEC primacy, the Big Ten is winning the meta. Three CFP semifinalists last year, a national title, and a recruiting map that no longer ends at the Mason-Dixon line.

latest_chapter.title: Spring 2026: Three's the Question, and the Sherrone Moore Year
latest_chapter.dek: Two cycles after Michigan's title, the conference is structurally elevated and now confronting the chapter every elevated conference eventually confronts: the third trophy is the proof.
latest_chapter.byline: From The Big Ten Reasserting Department

threads.title: The 12-Team Playoff Settling
threads.dek: Two cycles into the bracket. The regular season is reshaping in ways the format's architects didn't fully predict — and the discourse around it has gone exactly where it always goes.

latest_chapter.title: Two Cycles In: The November That No Longer Eliminates
latest_chapter.dek: After two seasons of the 12-team bracket, the regular season has settled into a new rhythm — and the part the architects didn't fully predict is what the rhythm has done to October.

threads.title: Realignment Endgame
threads.dek: Pac-12 dissolved. SEC and Big Ten ate the country. The ACC is the next domino. The slow-motion train of CFB's geography keeps moving, and the chapters keep landing.

latest_chapter.title: Spring 2026: The ACC Settles, Yormark Holds, and Notre Dame Stays Independent for Now
latest_chapter.dek: Three realignment chapters compounding. The ACC's lawsuit settled in March. The Big 12's PE deal closed in February. Notre Dame extended its independence agreement with NBC. The endgame is no longer a singular event; it is a steady-state condition.

threads.title: The Portal Era Settling
threads.dek: Four years deep into the transfer portal. The early chaos is yielding to patterns — who wins, who loses, who learned to navigate, and which programs treat the portal as a strategy rather than an emergency.

latest_chapter.title: Spring 2026: Portal-Evaluation as a Coaching Specialty, the Receivers Gold Rush, and the Next Cignettis
latest_chapter.dek: Four years deep into the portal era. The chaos hypothesis is dead. The architecture practitioners have been identified. The next coaching specialty is portal-evaluation: the work of figuring out which players will translate.
latest_chapter.byline: From The Portal Era Settling Department

threads.title: ND-USC: A Rivalry Recalibrating
threads.dek: Two blue bloods, separated by realignment, a coastal divide, and a hundred and thirty years of memory. The series resumes — and the discourse about what it should be is louder than the game.

latest_chapter.title: Spring 2026: The Series Holds, the Programs Diverge Again, and the Rivalry's Future Is the Schedule's Discipline
latest_chapter.dek: Two cycles of post-realignment ND-USC are in the books. The series is intact. The future depends on whether USC and Notre Dame keep deciding it should be intact.
latest_chapter.byline: From The ND-USC: A Rivalry Recalibrating Department

threads.title: The Saban-to-DeBoer Transition
threads.dek: Eighteen months into Alabama after Saban. The question is no longer whether DeBoer can win — it's what Bama becomes when the founder is gone and the standard remains.

latest_chapter.title: Spring 2026: The Standard, Re-Articulated
latest_chapter.dek: Two cycles in. DeBoer's identity is showing. The OL has been rebuilt. The third recruiting class has landed. The Standard is being re-articulated in DeBoer's voice. The question is no longer whether it holds. The question is what it sounds like now.
